# Make One Sound

## Lesson Goal

Generate a sine wave, write it as audio, and display it in Marimo.

## What to Notice

A notebook can be a small reproducible instrument: code creates an artifact and the browser immediately makes it inspectable.

## Work With the Notebook

- [Open the notebook source](https://github.com/buildLittleWorlds/marimo-audio-self-tutorial/blob/main/notebooks/01_sine_wave/notebook.py)
- [Run the browser notebook](https://buildlittleworlds.github.io/marimo-audio-self-tutorial/notebooks/01_sine_wave/)

Run locally:

```bash
uv run marimo edit notebooks/01_sine_wave/notebook.py
```

## Feedback Lens

Look for whether students can point to the exact variables that define the sound: sample rate, frequency, duration, and amplitude.

## Instructor Notes

Use this lesson as a small checkpoint. Before asking students to add features,
ask them to name the input, the transformation, the output, and the current
limitation. That habit will matter more in later transcription notebooks than
any one line of code.
